{{Infobox event
| name = Bleeding Phoenix
| introduced = cradle-04-skysworn
}}

''See also: [[Bleeding Phoenix Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The Bleeding [[Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] characterized by its composition of liquid [[blood madra]] and [[hunger madra]]. Its physical form is described as a massive, semi-liquid crimson entity that can span an entire horizon. This creature possesses a unique method of regeneration; it disperses fragments known as [[Blood Shadows]] throughout the world, which act as hunger spirits to steal power from others and return it to the [[Phoenix]]. This process allows the [[Dreadgod]] to eventually rebuild its body from the gathered energy. While [[Monarchs]] can temporarily destroy its physical form, it inevitably reforms. [[The Phoenix]] is supported by the [[Redmoon Hall]], a cult of followers and scavengers that attend to its needs. Some members of this group seek out [[Blood Shadows]] to gain power, while others view the entity with religious devotion. The creature's awakening can be sudden and devastating, often bypassing the long-term premonitions typically used by [[sacred artists]] to prepare for [[Dreadgod attacks]]. Its presence is marked by a red sky, crimson lightning, and the spreading of its eggs, which can merge with various hosts to create bloodspawn. Beyond its destructive potential, the [[Phoenix]] is noted for a role in restoring order to worlds, though this restoration only returns things to a previous state rather than preventing eventual decay.}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The Bleeding [[Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] whose consciousness is scattered across thousands of pieces, making it a fragment sought after by the [[Dreadgod cult]]. Resting hundreds of miles to the south beneath a city of tattered cloth, this entity can be alerted by a breach in its seal. It was eventually awakened by the presence of power within the same [[labyrinth]] containing the [[Ancestor's Spear]] and [[hunger madra]] [[binding]]. This massive event was not predicted until only weeks before it occurred, at which point the entity offered mercy. Upon its rise, the Bleeding [[Phoenix]] spread its wings like a wound stretching from one horizon to another, causing Arelius branches in the south to close.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] currently within range.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c2">''Skysworn'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] rests hundreds of miles to the south beneath a city of tattered cloth.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] offers mercy.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c9">''Skysworn'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a fragment sought out by the [[Dreadgod cult]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c10">''Skysworn'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is an entity that can be alerted by a breach in a seal.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11">''Skysworn'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The rise of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] has closed Arelius branches in the south.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c16">''Skysworn'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] spread its wings like a wound stretching from one horizon to another.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] was awakened by power in the same [[labyrinth]] as the [[Ancestor's Spear]] and [[hunger madra]] [[binding]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a massive event or entity that was not predicted until weeks before it happened.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]]'s consciousness is scattered over thousands of pieces.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18" />}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The passage of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] left buildings in the town half-destroyed.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c1">''Ghostwater'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The passage of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] caused a disturbance around the facility.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c3">''Ghostwater'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] that recently awoke.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c14">''Ghostwater'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] caused devastation to the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4">''Underlord'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|One dream tablet comes from a researcher who examined the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] directly.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5">''Underlord'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is beginning to go dormant.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c22">''Underlord'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a crimson bird with wings spread.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c10">''Uncrowned'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] covered the sky from horizon to horizon.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c19">''Uncrowned'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Originally established as a cult by the [[Blood Sage]], the Bleeding [[Phoenix]] is also identified as a massive [[Dreadgod]] capable of spanning an entire horizon. Although founded by the [[Sage]], the entity maintains no influence over him, even as the [[Blood Shadow]] returns stolen power to it. The name serves as a symbol on [[Redmoon Hall]]'s cloudship and is recognized by the [[Sage of Red Faith]] as a specific lineage or group. This influential organization faces the potential extermination of its [[Dreadgod]] form by [[Reigan Shen]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a cult founded by the [[Blood Sage]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7">''Wintersteel'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] that can take up an entire horizon.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9">''Wintersteel'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blood Sage]] has no influence over the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c11">''Wintersteel'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blood Shadow]] brings stolen power back to the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17">''Wintersteel'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a symbol on [[Redmoon Hall]]'s cloudship.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c18">''Wintersteel'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a group or lineage mentioned by the [[Sage of Red Faith]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c19">''Wintersteel'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c23">''Wintersteel'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Reigan Shen]] may exterminate the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c27">''Wintersteel'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a group that exerts influence.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The Bleeding [[Phoenix]] is a phenomenon that [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] survived during its initial rampage, an event characterized by an enhanced blood [[aura]] that turned the sky red. After being witnessed within the [[Blackflame Empire]], the entity was managed by [[Akura Malice]], who had faced it previously and successfully kept it away from specific areas for several days. While [[Eithan]] conducted experiments on the entity within a [[labyrinth]], its arrival at Heaven’s Glory frightened away those attempting to depart. The Bleeding [[Phoenix]] eventually flew into the [[Trackless Sea]], exhibiting behavioral deviations as it acted according to an unknown purpose.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] was a rampage that [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] lived through.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4">''Bloodline'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] enhanced blood [[aura]] to make the sky blaze red.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] was witnessed in the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9">''Bloodline'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is an entity that [[Akura Malice]] was able to keep away from certain areas for a few days.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14">''Bloodline'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Eithan]] performed experiments on the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] in the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Akura Malice]] had previously faced the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15">''Bloodline'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The arrival of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] scared away people attempting to leave through Heaven’s Glory.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a location [[Yerin]] might have tossed herself to.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is flying into the [[Trackless Sea]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21">''Bloodline'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] has exhibited behavioral deviation and is acting according to an unknown purpose.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21" />}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The Bleeding [[Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] composed of blood and [[madra]] with an oozing red body. Born from the bone ring, this entity possesses a scattered awareness that feels like a council within its mind. It is characterized by an [[aura]] of hunger, corruption, and death, with its [[essence]] being compared to certain tanks and ghouls. This creature can call down a rain of red lightning and has the ability to dissolve its form into droplets that act as eggs through spatial distortion. Though [[Akura Malice]] saw no signs of its rise, the Bleeding [[Phoenix]] eventually emitted a keening cry and fled.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] with an oozing red body.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2">''Reaper'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is an event that [[Akura Malice]] has not seen hints of rising.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9">''Reaper'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The ghouls are compared to the bloodspawn of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c12">''Reaper'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The creature in the chamber above had a sense of hunger, corruption, and death like the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c14">''Reaper'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The tanks gave off an impression similar to the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c19">''Reaper'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The bone ring once gave birth to the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] that can call a rain of red lightning.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24">''Reaper'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]]'s awareness is scattered, feeling like a council in its head.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is made of blood and [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] can dissolve its body into droplets that act as eggs through spatial distortion.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] gave a keening cry and fled.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The Bleeding [[Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] composed of blood and hunger aspects that flies east through the [[Trackless Sea]] for [[Iceflower]]. This phoenix is a figure of horror stories whose [[aura]] stains the clouds with crimson stripes, and it possesses the ability to exert pressure on others through its song. While traveling across an entire continent alone, the entity maintains contact with [[Redmoon]] and appears to be bleeding because it keeps other aspects trapped in semi-gelatinous spheres. During a confrontation, the creature produces a tiny avatar made of blood featuring a liquid beak, only for the [[Sage of Red Faith]] to unleash everything into the beast.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a [[Dreadgod]] flying east through the [[Trackless Sea]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2">''Dreadgod'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]]'s [[aura]] stains the world with crimson stripes across the clouds.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is associated with horror stories.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is flying for [[Iceflower]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4">''Dreadgod'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] has an entire continent to itself.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is composed of blood and hunger aspects.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c9">''Dreadgod'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] appears to be bleeding because it keeps other aspects in semi-gelatinous spheres.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is an entity that [[Redmoon]] has been in contact with.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c14">''Dreadgod'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a [[Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] can use its song to exert pressure on others.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] produces a tiny phoenix avatar from blood.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]]'s avatar has a liquid beak.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Sage of Red Faith]] unleashes everything into the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The Bleeding [[Phoenix]] serves as a source of [[madra]] within [[Yerin]], though she initially denied its will. While the [[Monarchs]] are responsible for keeping the entity alive, it possesses a song and could potentially serve as a perfect weapon for [[Yerin]]. This entity can manifest as a red-haired woman with scarlet wings and has begun consuming the people of [[Emriss Silentborn]] by merging its eggs with humans, animals, [[sacred beasts]], and trees. During a massive battle felt globally by the advanced, its power was sensed like a crimson bonfire as it attacked [[Lindon]] with ten thousand needles and a barrage of [[Striker techniques]]. Although the entity retaliated against the [[Phoenix]], the tide of combat turned toward [[Lindon]] after [[Shen]]'s departure. Ultimately, the creature's core binding resembles a sealed egg, and its cloak was capable of swallowing [[Li Markuth]]'s [[sword]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Yerin]] denied the will of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is a source of [[madra]] inside [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c8">''Waybound'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Monarchs]] are responsible for the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] staying alive.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12">''Waybound'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] has a song.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] would be a perfect weapon for [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21">''Waybound'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is taking [[Emriss Silentborn]]'s people.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24">''Waybound'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]]'s eggs have merged with trees, animals, [[sacred beasts]], and humans.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] can take the form of a human woman with bright red hair and scarlet wings.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] might take care of [[Reigan Shen]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] sends ten thousand needles at [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c27">''Waybound'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] uses a barrage of [[Striker techniques]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c28">''Waybound'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The battle between [[Dreadgods]] can be felt by everyone in the world with sufficient advancement.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]]'s power is sensed like a crimson bonfire.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The battle against the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] tilted toward [[Lindon]] after [[Shen]] was gone.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30">''Waybound'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] retaliated against the [[Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The core binding of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] resembles a sealed egg.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c34">''Waybound'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The cloak of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] swallowed [[Li Markuth]]'s [[sword]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35">''Waybound'', chapter 35</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The cult of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] is protected by [[Yerin Arelius]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c3">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The cloak of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] billowed from [[Lindon]]'s shoulders.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Bleeding Phoenix]] rose after a few days.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|“We are responding to reports of a disturbance around this facility after the passage of the Bleeding Phoenix.”<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“Northstrider told me that my people were being taken by the Bleeding Phoenix. I assumed he was lying, but there was always the chance.”<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“If we’re lucky, the Bleeding Phoenix will take care of Shen for us,” Emriss said.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|“The cult of the Bleeding Phoenix is protected by Yerin Arelius, and I carry her burdens now.”<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c3" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Events]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle events]]
